templates: {name} has run {business} for {years} years, ever since {origin}. {personality_line} {hook}
Everyone in {town} knows {name}. {business} has been in {pronoun_pos} family for {years} years. {personality_line} {hook}
{name} came to {town} as a stranger and never left. {pronoun} bought {business} from the previous owner and hasn't looked back. {personality_line} {hook}
origins: inheriting it from a distant aunt
winning it in a card game
buying it with savings from years of travel
a partner convinced {pronoun} to settle down
fleeing a past {pronoun} doesn't speak of

personality_lines.brave: {pronoun_cap} has been known to throw out armed men with {pronoun_pos} bare hands.
personality_lines.cautious: {pronoun_cap} keeps a short blade under the bar, just in case.
personality_lines.greedy: The prices at {business} are fair — just barely.
personality_lines.generous: {pronoun_cap} has been known to let travelers sleep in the stable for free on cold nights.
personality_lines.curious: {pronoun_cap} listens more than most people realize.
personality_lines.suspicious: {pronoun_cap} trusts nobody who doesn't eat before they drink.
personality_lines.loyal: The regulars at {business} would do anything for {pronoun_obj}.
personality_lines.pious: {pronoun_cap} keeps a small shrine to the church behind the bar.
personality_lines.cynical: {pronoun_cap} has heard every hard-luck story twice.
personality_lines.cheerful: The warmth at {business} is as much {pronoun_pos} doing as the fire's.

hooks: Lately {pronoun} has been sleeping poorly — something about a guest who checked in three weeks ago and never left.
There's a room on the top floor {pronoun} won't rent out, and nobody asks why.
{pronoun_cap} owes a favor to someone {pronoun} hopes never collects.
A particular face has been watching {business} from across the square each evening.
The cellar goes deeper than the floor plan suggests.
